summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 11 warning, 10 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: The remaining open items from prior review passes (security ЁЯЯб for undocumented `shell:true` trust model, security ЁЯЯб for missing artifact-count cap, tester ЁЯЯб for two test-coverage gaps) are all backlog items тАФ none represent functional defects in the stated requirement.

ЁЯЯб `synthesize.mjs:127` тАФ `verdictAppend` per-finding guard accepts `typeof f.text === "string"` without requiring non-empty; a buggy extension returning `{ severity: "critical", text: "" }` produces a FAIL verdict with a blank finding entry and no diagnostic text тАФ add `f.text.trim().length > 0` to the guard

ЁЯЯб `run.mjs:363-368` тАФ When `writeFileSync` throws, no content file is created but the descriptor is still appended to `extras` and merged into handshake.json; this write-failure case is behaviorally identical to the intentional no-`content` case and is undocumented тАФ document or branch on write success to distinguish "pre-existing path reference" from "write failed silently"

**Summary:** Implementation is correct. Core flow traced end-to-end: `runArtifactEmit` writes content files and returns task-dir-relative descriptors; `mergeExtrasIntoHandshake` is called after the gate handshake is written (correct ordering); review handshakes at `run.mjs:1455` and `1533` both include `allExtras` via `buildReviewArtifacts`; scoping of `allExtras` inside the retry loop prevents cross-attempt accumulation; `resetRegistry()` at feature start prevents extension leakage; `parseFindings` false-positive fix confirmed via `startsWith` and passing regression tests. Two ЁЯЯб warnings go to backlog. No critical issues.
## Prior ЁЯЯб Findings тАФ All Resolved

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/extension-runner.mjs:27` тАФ Timeout path never tested; a never-resolving hook should return null and increment the failure counter тАФ add test with fake/shortened timeout to verify the Promise.race resolution

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/run.mjs:380` тАФ `mergeExtrasIntoHandshake` silently no-ops when the handshake file doesn't exist with non-empty extras; extension artifacts are silently lost with no signal тАФ add a test documenting this contract

**Summary:** The core implementation is correct and well-tested at the unit level. All stated sub-requirements are verified directly in code: `runArtifactEmit` writes content with a 10 MB cap, `mergeExtrasIntoHandshake` is called at the gate handshake write site and is tested using the production function, and `buildReviewArtifacts(allExtras)` appears at all three review handshake write paths with disk-readback regression coverage. The two ЁЯЯб items (timeout path unverified, merge silent-discard undocumented) are backlog coverage gaps тАФ neither represents a functional defect in the stated requirement.

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/run.mjs:305` тАФ `execSync(cmd, { shell: true })` runs extension-provided commands with full shell expansion and no allowlist; document the extension trust model in the `fireExtension` JSDoc (only install extensions from trusted sources), and consider `shell: false` with explicit arg splitting as defense-in-depth

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/run.mjs:353` тАФ `runArtifactEmit` has no artifact-count cap; a malicious extension returning a very large `artifacts` array causes unbounded disk writes тАФ add a per-call ceiling (e.g., skip after 50 entries) before the loop

ЁЯЯб `bin/lib/run.mjs:344` тАФ JSDoc note "callers can register paths for pre-existing files" is misleading: the index-prefix renaming at line 361 (`safeName = \`${idx++}-${base}\``) makes the registered descriptor path unpredictable for content-less entries; an extension cannot know what prefixed filename will be generated for a file it wrote independently тАФ remove or replace this documented use case with accurate language
No ЁЯФ┤ critical findings in any of the four veto categories:
